startups.name: Alpha Labs
startups.one_liner: Test fixture: strong team, weak traction.

founder_market_fit.reasoning: From founder LinkedIn showing 8 years building the exact product category at Stripe, we can infer maximum founder-market fit.

technical_depth.reasoning: From a founder bio listing Google staff engineer and an OSS project with 30k GitHub stars, we can infer world-class technical depth.

prior_founding_experience.reasoning: From Crunchbase showing a prior $400M exit, we can infer top-tier founding experience.

team_completeness.reasoning: From the team page listing CEO + CTO + Head of Sales, we can infer the core functions are covered but design is missing.

network_credibility.reasoning: From the funding announcement listing Sequoia lead + 5 named tier-1 angels, we can infer top-tier network credibility.

tam_size.reasoning: From Gartner's report citing a $40B segment, we can infer a large but not category-leading TAM.

growth_rate.reasoning: From the same Gartner report showing 28% CAGR, we can infer strong but not extreme growth.

timing.reasoning: From the recent regulatory shift opening this category, we can infer favorable timing.

competitive_intensity_inv.reasoning: From a competitive landscape showing 8 funded competitors, we can infer moderate competitive intensity.

differentiation.reasoning: From the changelog and demo showing a unique data-pipeline approach absent in competitors, we can infer strong differentiation.

technical_moat.reasoning: From a patent filing on the core algorithm, we can infer real technical moat.

velocity.reasoning: From a public changelog showing weekly releases, we can infer high product velocity.

defensibility.reasoning: From the product being self-hosted with no data lock-in, we can infer moderate defensibility only.

revenue_signal.reasoning: From a press release mentioning 3 design partners but no ARR figure, we can infer early but unproven revenue.

users_customers.reasoning: From only 3 named design partners and no waitlist disclosure, we can infer thin user traction.

growth_rate.reasoning: From the absence of any growth metric disclosure, we can infer growth is too early to measure.

engagement_retention.reasoning: From a single quoted customer testimonial and no retention metric, we can infer engagement is unproven.

startups.name: Bravo Inc
startups.one_liner: Test fixture: balanced profile, partial data.

founder_market_fit.reasoning: From a founder bio showing adjacent (not direct) industry experience, we can infer moderate fit.

technical_depth.reasoning: From the team page listing two senior engineers from a known infra company, we can infer strong technical depth.

team_completeness.reasoning: From the team page showing technical co-founders only with no commercial hire, we can infer a coverage gap.

network_credibility.reasoning: From a single named angel investor and no accelerator, we can infer modest credibility.

tam_size.reasoning: From an analyst report citing a $120B segment, we can infer maximum TAM.

growth_rate.reasoning: From the same analyst report citing 45% CAGR, we can infer maximum growth.

timing.reasoning: From recent customer behavior shifts in the segment, we can infer favorable timing.

differentiation.reasoning: From the landing page showing similar feature set to competitors, we can infer modest differentiation.

technical_moat.reasoning: From the absence of a patent or unique architecture mentioned, we can infer no clear moat.

velocity.reasoning: From a GitHub repo showing daily commits for 6 months, we can infer high velocity.

defensibility.reasoning: From the product being a CLI with portable data, we can infer moderate defensibility.

revenue_signal.reasoning: From the absence of any paid customer mention and stealth status, we can infer no revenue signal.

users_customers.reasoning: From a Discord community of 200 members, we can infer light early-user signal.

startups.name: Charlie Co

tam_size.reasoning: From an analyst figure of ~$8B for this niche, we can infer mid-size TAM.

growth_rate.reasoning: From the same analyst figure showing ~15% CAGR, we can infer moderate growth.

timing.reasoning: From no obvious tailwind or headwind in the segment, we can infer neutral timing.

competitive_intensity_inv.reasoning: From a handful of established competitors and few new entrants, we can infer moderate competitive intensity.

differentiation.reasoning: From the landing page showing one novel feature on top of a standard offering, we can infer modest differentiation.

technical_moat.reasoning: From the absence of unique IP but a complex integration surface, we can infer modest moat.

velocity.reasoning: From monthly product updates on the blog, we can infer steady velocity.

defensibility.reasoning: From moderate switching costs once integrated, we can infer moderate defensibility.

revenue_signal.reasoning: From a press release mentioning paying pilots, we can infer early revenue.

users_customers.reasoning: From a customer-logos page showing 8 mid-market companies, we can infer real early-adopter traction.

growth_rate.reasoning: From a founder tweet citing 'doubled in 6 months', we can infer healthy growth (~12% MoM).

engagement_retention.reasoning: From a testimonial citing 'core daily tool', we can infer reasonable engagement.

startups.notes: Used to verify the missing-pillar warning path.
